description: v2 link-tier wrap binds the AEAD AAD to (link_id, tier, epoch_id) so a malicious server cannot substitute wrap rows across tiers, links, or epochs. The AAD is the byte-exact concatenation 'mosaic:l3-link-tier-key:v2' (26 bytes) || link_id (16 bytes) || tier_byte (1 byte) || epoch_id_be (4 bytes) = 47 bytes total. Rust is the canonical reference; WASM and UniFFI exports call the same crypto-core function. Wrap is non-deterministic (24-byte random nonce); this vector locks the AAD construction bytes (which deterministically derive from inputs) and exercises round-trip wrap+unwrap via the v2 API. Closes audit 'share-link-create C1'.

negativeCases.name: tier-mismatch
mutations: wrap with tier=3 then unwrap with expected_tier=2

negativeCases.name: wrong-epoch-id
mutations: wrap with epoch_id=42 then unwrap with epoch_id=43
negativeCases.rationale: The v2 AAD includes epoch_id_be — a server presenting a wrap from a different epoch fails the AEAD check.

negativeCases.name: wrong-link-id
mutations: wrap with link_secret A then unwrap with derived link_id from link_secret B
negativeCases.rationale: The v2 AAD includes the 16-byte link_id — a server cross-mounting a wrap from another share link fails the AEAD check.

negativeCases.name: tier-substitution-attack
mutations: wrap tier=3 (full) under link X, then present the same ciphertext labelled tier=1 (thumb) to a thumb-only visitor
negativeCases.rationale: The v2 AAD includes tier_byte — a server substituting a higher-tier wrap fails the visitor's AEAD check (audit 'share-link-create C1').

negativeCases.name: v1-reader-rejects-v2-wrap
mutations: call unwrap_tier_key_from_link (v1) on a v2-bound ciphertext
negativeCases.rationale: No-silent-downgrade mirror property — a stale v1-only client must not transparently accept v2 wraps.

compatibility.dualAccept: v2 unwrap (unwrap_tier_key_from_link_v2) dual-accepts v1 wraps for back-compat with pre-A1 share links. v1 unwrap rejects v2 wraps.

serverBoundOutputs.name: wrappedTierKey
serverBoundOutputs.reason: Wrapped tier key is stored on the server alongside the link record.

serverBoundOutputs.name: tier
serverBoundOutputs.reason: Access tier byte is part of the link manifest record.

serverBoundOutputs.name: epochId
serverBoundOutputs.reason: Epoch identifier is part of the album manifest and visible to the server.

forbiddenServerOutputs.name: tierKey
forbiddenServerOutputs.reason: Plaintext tier keys must never appear server-side.

forbiddenServerOutputs.name: wrappingKey
forbiddenServerOutputs.reason: Derived wrapping key must stay client-side.
